decision.summary: Ecommerce business coverage is stable for this growth-stage business. Auretix would stay in monitoring mode and protect working capital. Scenario mode: normal conditions.

metrics.label: Objective mode
metrics.detail: This tells Auretix which tradeoff to favor when recommendations compete with each other.

metrics.label: Scenario mode
metrics.detail: This tells Auretix whether to simulate normal conditions, supplier delays, or demand spikes.

metrics.label: Launch and promotion pressure
metrics.detail: This is the business-specific operating pressure Auretix is layering into the base procurement and supply-chain model.

metrics.label: Urgency
metrics.detail: Auretix uses urgency to decide how much hands-on support this business should receive.

actions: Keep purchasing discipline tight on top SKUs and review supplier timing before the next growth push.

Target NaN units on the next buying cycle to preserve service levels without overbuying. Recommended procurement posture: prioritize velocity SKUs first, then size the PO to protect growth without overbuying.
Protect reorder timing on top SKUs and avoid starving growth during momentum.
Guard in-stock continuity across key sales channels before optimizing for long-tail efficiency.
This business needs decisions that keep growth moving without creating operational chaos.
Current objective mode: protect service levels.
Scenario mode: normal conditions.
Launch and promotion pressure: 30/100. Auretix is using this business-specific signal to shape the recommendation.
Margin is healthy enough to support growth, but only if purchasing timing stays disciplined.

supportTier.reason: Best for businesses that mainly need automated reorder timing, risk alerts, and lightweight guidance.

points: Recommended PO size: NaN units.

panels.title: Supply chain
points: Projected cover: NaN days against a 28-day lead time.
Guard in-stock continuity across key sales channels before optimizing for long-tail efficiency.
Auretix would watch ranking-sensitive SKUs and channel availability more aggressively than slower-moving tail inventory.

Launch and promotion pressure is currently adding extra pressure to the operational plan.
Current buffer is still covering the inbound window, but momentum needs monitoring.

panels.title: Unified decision
points: Keep purchasing discipline tight on top SKUs and review supplier timing before the next growth push.
Auretix would favor quick reorder clarity and service continuity because lost stock can kill momentum fast.
Decision objective: protect service levels.
Scenario mode: normal conditions.
Auretix support recommendation: Starter.
Main tradeoff: balancing growth with inventory discipline.

items.name: Launch SKU
riskReasons: Launch SKU is below its reorder point, so available coverage is already tighter than the replenishment plan wants.
Pacific Components is only 68% reliable in this scenario, which increases the odds that inbound timing slips when you need it most.
1 open PO line is already carrying supplier risk, so this item has active execution exposure, not just forecast risk.
items.playbookSummary: Fund this item on purpose because it has the right mix of demand, margin, and upside.
playbookReasons: Launch SKU combines healthy margin and strong capital efficiency, so it deserves more deliberate funding.
Launch SKU is converting inventory into profitable growth faster than the portfolio average.
items.action: Keep Launch SKU funded on purpose and give it enough runway to capture profitable demand.

portfolioRecommendations: Portfolio recommendation: protect continuity on operationally critical items first, then grow the products that return cash efficiently.
Playbook recommendation: 0 items are in Protect, 3 are in Grow, 3 are in Fix, and 2 are in Deprioritize.
Margin recommendation: Tail SKU is your weakest gross-margin item, so it deserves extra caution before more inventory is committed.
Inbound recommendation: 5 tracked items have inbound arriving within the next 14 days, so timing discipline matters right now.
constraintRecommendations: Cash constraint: the recommended plan is $838 above the current cash-safe budget, so approvals should be sequenced instead of released all at once.
Warehouse constraint: the recommended plan would overrun current storage by 6831 units, so the brain should slow lower-priority buys or stage arrivals.
Supplier constraint: the weakest supplier lane is 702 units short of the modeled demand plan, so a split PO or alternate source is needed.
MOQ constraint: priority buying is clustering around a 405-unit minimum, so smaller corrective moves may need bundling or supplier negotiation.
Terms constraint: Supplier deposit terms are front-loading cash before inventory turns.

planningBoard.focus: Close the immediate inventory and supplier gaps that could hit revenue first.
outcomes: 0 products should be protected inside the next buying window.
Approved spend should stay inside $39577 unless the business is explicitly prioritizing growth.
Pacific Components is the weakest supplier link inside the near-term plan.

planningBoard.focus: Repair margin leaks and rebalance supply so the portfolio stops over-funding weak items.
outcomes: 3 products need procurement, margin, or supplier fixes before scaling harder.
2 products should stay lean while stronger items absorb working capital first.
Projected footprint reaches 8822 units if every recommendation is funded.

planningBoard.focus: Choose which products earn future capital and which channels deserve deeper inventory confidence.
outcomes: 3 products currently qualify for deliberate scale funding.
Scenario planning remains anchored to normal conditions, so future commitments should be stress-tested before they are locked in.
Protect service now transitions from tactical firefighting into a repeatable operating rhythm by this horizon.

portfolioRecommendations: Portfolio recommendation: protect continuity on operationally critical items first, then grow the products that return cash efficiently.
Playbook recommendation: 0 items are in Protect, 3 are in Grow, 3 are in Fix, and 2 are in Deprioritize.

Inbound recommendation: 5 tracked items have inbound arriving within the next 14 days, so timing discipline matters right now.
constraintRecommendations: Cash constraint: the recommended plan is $838 above the current cash-safe budget, so approvals should be sequenced instead of released all at once.
Warehouse constraint: the recommended plan would overrun current storage by 6831 units, so the brain should slow lower-priority buys or stage arrivals.
Supplier constraint: the weakest supplier lane is 702 units short of the modeled demand plan, so a split PO or alternate source is needed.
MOQ constraint: priority buying is clustering around a 405-unit minimum, so smaller corrective moves may need bundling or supplier negotiation.
Terms constraint: Supplier deposit terms are front-loading cash before inventory turns.
